sources.name: The Woks of Life
sources.notes: Family-run Chinese cooking blog. Authentic recipes with accessible ingredients, good pantry guides.

sources.name: Just One Cookbook
sources.notes: Nami's Japanese home cooking. Clear step-by-step, great for everyday washoku.

sources.name: Serious Eats
sources.notes: Technique-forward, well-tested. Kenji Lopez-Alt's stuff is especially reliable.

sources.name: BBC Good Food
sources.notes: Huge searchable catalog, filterable by time/difficulty. Good for quick weeknight ideas.

sources.name: NYT Cooking
sources.notes: High editorial quality. Paywalled — needs a subscription.

sources.name: Budget Bytes
sources.notes: Cost-per-serving included. Great for cheap, simple weeknight meals.

sources.name: Smitten Kitchen
sources.notes: Deb Perelman's comfort-food angle. Strong on baking and vegetarian.
